heroImageAlt: A patient speaking with a bereavement support team member in a calm hospice setting
heroShortDescription: Bereavement Support for individuals and extended family members
mainIntro1: We offer compassionate support for individuals and families experiencing bereavement or anticipatory bereavement — including those affected by loss, serious illness, or caring for someone with palliative needs. Whether you are already known to our hospice or are contacting us for the first time, we are here to listen, support, and guide you.
mainIntro2: Support is available face-to-face or remotely.

whoList.text: Individuals experiencing anticipatory bereavement, including those caring for or supporting someone with a palliative or life-limiting illness
People who have been recently bereaved or bereaved some time ago
Family members, carers, and loved ones
Patients and carers already known to our hospice
Anyone making an enquiry for the first time

callout: There is no “right” time to seek support. Whether your loss has already happened or is something you are living with and preparing for, we are here to support you.
anticipatoryBody1: Anticipatory bereavement is the grief and emotional distress that can be experienced before a death, when someone is living with or caring for a person with a life-limiting or palliative condition.
anticipatoryBody2: These feelings may include sadness, anxiety, exhaustion, fear, or a sense of loss for the future that was hoped for. Anticipatory bereavement is a normal and valid experience, and support can be helpful at any stage — not just after someone has died.
nurseIntro: You can access informal bereavement support from our trained Palliative Care and Bereavement Support Nurse.

nurseList.text: A compassionate and non-judgmental listening ear
Practical advice around coping with grief
Signposting to helpful services and resources

nurseFoot: Support is available face-to-face at the hospice or remotely (by phone or video).
dropInIntro: We are currently in the process of setting up the following support groups:

bullets.text: Open to anyone who is bereaved, at any stage
A relaxed, supportive space to share experiences or simply listen
For men who are bereaved, supporting or caring for someone with a palliative condition, or living with a palliative condition themselves
An informal group offering connection and peer support

dropInFoot: Dates and times will be confirmed soon. Please complete the enquiry form to register your interest.
counsellingBody: For some people, more structured support may be helpful. Our counselling service is available through referral from our Bereavement Support Nurse.
counsellingCta: For more details about our counselling service click here.
sideFormHint: Register interest for support groups, or ask about bereavement support.

afterIntro.alt: A patient speaking with a bereavement support team member in a calm hospice setting
